page: Amazon Web Services i8g.12xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance provides 48 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The CPU features 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, translating to 8.0 GB per core, and includes 11250 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 28 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals top-tier performance in memory operations and database workloads. The server achieved a top-tier rating in passmark database operations and passmark memory score. Memory bandwidth tests show top-tier results for large block read-only and write operations. Multi-core processing is strong, with a Geekbench multi-core score and a clang software compilation score both placing in the top tier. Static web serving throughput also reached a top-tier level of 102.83 GB/sec.

While multi-core and database performance are highly competitive, single-core metrics are more moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and passmark CPU encryption scoring in the average tier. Additionally, LLM text generation for small models is weak, though text generation for medium and large models remains strong. Given the massive local NVMe SSD capacity and high memory bandwidth, this instance is optimized for database operations, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and data-intensive storage workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services i8g.12xlarge is an arm64 storage-optimized server utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of memory and 11250 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance delivers top-tier performance in database operations, memory bandwidth, and software compilation, alongside strong multi-core capabilities. However, single-core CPU performance and small-model LLM text generation remain in the average and weak tiers, respectively. With a baseline network bandwidth of 28 Gbps and high resource density, this server is designed for high-throughput database systems, large-scale web serving, and storage-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.12xlarge is an arm64 storage-optimized server with 48 Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and 11250 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and memory bandwidth performance.

tagline: An arm64 storage-optimized instance featuring massive local NVMe SSD capacity, high memory bandwidth, and dedicated physical cores for database workloads.

11250 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-speed data access.
384.0 GB of system memory delivering top-tier read and write bandwidth.
Top-tier performance in database operations and software compilation.
28 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed via the Nitro hypervisor.
Optimized for high-throughput databases, web serving, and storage-heavy workloads.
